restaurants.name: Saint Urban -2026
restaurants.excerpt: Once in a lifetime type experience!
restaurants.review:
2026 has been a tough year. We lost my brother — a man who loved New York City and called it home for over twenty five years. We came back to the city he loved to celebrate his life. And afterwards, Rachel and I did what we always do — we leaned on each other, took a breath, and tried to find a moment of beauty in a heavy time.


We had celebrated our seventeenth wedding anniversary a few weeks before but never really did anything special. So I seized the moment. We frantically googled the best wine restaurants in New York City and Saint Urban popped up. Never heard of it. Decided to give it a shot. Hopped in a cab, made a last minute reservation, honestly surprised I got in and twenty minutes later we were walking through the door.


The moment we sat down we knew we were somewhere special. We did what we always do, sat at the bar. Nothing we enjoy more than dining at the bar. We were greeted warmly and almost immediately Jared Stafford-Hill came out, the chef and sommelier to say hello. I told him we had married in 2009 and would love to celebrate with a Burgundy from that vintage. He disappeared into his four thousand bottle wine cellar and came back with a 2009 Louis Saint-George, La Bousseauvre, Premier Cru. Three hundred dollars. I say that with a grin on my face for that vintage, that producer, that quality, at a restaurant in New York City? That is a steal.

What followed was a seven course prix fixe dinner. Each course more luscious and thoughtful than the last. Fancy but never pretentious. Done with care, done with love, with conversation woven through every pour. Jared joined us throughout the evening and we discovered we had visited the same wine bars in Burgundy and Beaune on separate trips. The world got very small in the best way.
And then toward the end of the evening a young couple sat down next to us. Reminded us of ourselves twenty years ago. We got to talking. Turns out they came to Saint Urban because they heard the wine was great and they wanted to learn. We talked wine, we talked life. And then it came out they grew up in my hometown. Same elementary school. Same middle school. Strangers at a wine bar in New York City who turned out to be practically neighbors.


That's what wine does. It brings people together in ways nothing else can. Where else does that happen?


## Travel Tips 

Reservations are highly recommended, don't try to walk in and wing it. That said, don't hesitate to sit at the bar if there's a spot. Engage Jared and his team, they are knowledgeable, passionate, and will help you navigate one of the most extraordinary wine lists you've ever seen. It comes on a large electronic pad that seamlessly goes on forever. Browse it, ask questions, trust them. This is a special occasion restaurant. Treat it like one.


## The Kazmi Take 

 **Of all the places I've been in New York, Saint Urban stands alone. It gave us exactly what we needed on one of the hardest trips we've ever taken, beauty, connection, and a glass of wine that meant something. We'll be back.**

restaurants.name: Le Bout Du Monde
restaurants.review:
Le Bout du Monde, Beaune, Burgundy
A few years ago Rachel and I found ourselves on our dream wine trip, starting in Lyon and making our way north through the Rhône Valley all the way up to Burgundy. It was the kind of trip you spend years planning in your head and then can't believe is actually happening. While dining at one of Lyon's many Michelin restaurants, our waiter mentioned a small wine bar we should check out when we reached Beaune. Nothing fancy he said, but a killer wine list. He called it Le Bout du Monde. I wrote it down, tucked it away, and didn't think much of it.


We made our way north through Pouilly, through the rolling countryside, and eventually found ourselves in Beaune. After our first full day of tastings, we had a couple of hours to spare before a dinner reservation I had made. Heads still in the clouds from sipping Burgundy all day, Rachel and I walked hand in hand toward this little wine bar. From the outside, nothing spectacular. You walk in and find a bar to the left, some couches, a bit of seating, and a cute little patio in the back. Exposed brick, simple high tops. We sat down not expecting much. Maybe just a glass of wine before dinner.
Then the waiter came over and handed us the wine list.


Pages upon pages upon pages of wine. Not just Burgundy, though they had Burgundy covered better than anywhere I had ever seen, vintages going back further than our budget could reach. Côtes du Rhône, Bordeaux, Cabernets, you name it, they had it. What we didn't know yet was that we were literally sitting on top of a cellar of thirty thousand bottles right beneath our feet. We ordered the 2016 Domaine Henri Delagrange Volnay 1er Cru, Champans. A Premier Cru Pinot Noir from one of Burgundy's most elegant villages, silky, refined, and absolutely perfect for a first night in Beaune. The food is simple, sandwiches, charcuterie, nothing elaborate, but more than enough to keep you grounded while you get lost in a wine list that could consume your entire evening.


One bottle became two. Dinner reservation canceled. We floated back to our beautiful second floor apartment in the middle of Beaune and called it a perfect night.


The next day, tastings done, evening coming, the question wasn't whether we were going back to Le Bout du Monde. It was whether we could pretend we had full intentions of going to dinner first. We ordered a small plate. Told ourselves it was just a quick stop. Then we decided to switch it up, stepped out of Burgundy for one night, and ordered the 2009 Closel-Roch Côte-Rôtie. A northern Rhône Syrah from a legendary vintage, smoky, layered, the kind of bottle that makes you put your fork down and just listen. Dinner was canceled again. We spent the entire night there.


By the third night the staff was greeting us like regulars. And that was the night we properly met Antoine, the owner. Warm, passionate, exactly the kind of person you want running a place like this. We got to talking and discovered that Antoine had done an internship at the University of Oklahoma. I never thought I would be sitting in a wine bar in Burgundy and hear the owner say Boomer Sooner. The world is a small and wonderful place. We were posing for photos, completely at home. For our third bottle we went back to Burgundy and ordered the 2014 Domaine Bernard & Thierry Glantenay Pommard 1er Cru, Les Saussilles. Pommard is the more structured, muscular cousin of Volnay, darker fruit, firmer tannins, and the Les Saussilles vineyard is one of the appellation's finest. Three nights, three Premier Crus, each one telling a different story of this magical region. We were becoming friends with Antoine and another dinner reservation quietly disappeared.
On the fourth night Rachel's friend joined us from Zurich. We had spent the day in Gevrey-Chambertin where I picked up a six pack of 2009 Grand Crus that I am still holding onto. Living on cloud nine doesn't begin to describe it. That evening was a Friday and the place was packed, but Antoine secured us a table without hesitation. The conversations flowed, the wine flowed, the room was buzzing with life. As we walked out that final night Antoine stopped us and offered a couple of cordials on the house. A thank you. A goodbye. Not a forever goodbye, just an until next time.


In all our years of traveling, Rachel and I have never gone to the same restaurant for dinner twice in a row, let alone four nights in a row. That should tell you everything you need to know about Le Bout du Monde.
The name translates literally to the end of the world. Frankly, if the world were ending, there is no place I would rather be, with my loved ones, with a glass of Burgundy, than right there.


##  Who Should Go 

Anyone who loves wine and wants to experience what it feels like to be completely lost in it. This is not a tourist stop. It is a pilgrimage. If you find yourself in Beaune, and you should find yourself in Beaune, this is your place.


## The Kazmi Take 

**Four nights in a row. Canceled dinner reservations we had spent weeks planning. Made a friend in Antoine that we will never forget. A Volnay, a Côte-Rôtie, and a Pommard, each one more memorable than the last. Le Bout du Monde is not just a wine bar. It is an experience that reminds you why wine is so much more than what is in the glass. Until next time, Antoine.** 🍷
